description: Canonical agent-hook policy for @engraph/castr. Guardrail-only: grounding reminder, quality-gate reminder, and blocking dangerous git/history-bypass/host-load commands. Each blocked entry carries a concept + citation + reappraisal so a deny TEACHES (PDR-044 §Innate immunity, as amended), enforced at commit-time by the validate-policy-reappraisal repo validator.

blocked_patterns.reappraisal: History here is append-only — never overwrite or rewrite it; integrate forward with new commits and reconcile branches without force-pushing or interactive rebase.
History here is append-only — never overwrite or rewrite it; integrate forward with new commits and reconcile branches without force-pushing or interactive rebase.
History here is append-only — a leased force-push still rewrites remote history; integrate forward with new commits and reconcile branches without force-pushing.
History here is append-only — never rewrite it with interactive rebase; layer corrections as new commits instead.
History here is append-only — never rewrite it with interactive rebase (long or short spelling); layer corrections as new commits instead.
History here is append-only and roll-forward only — amending rewrites the previous commit; land the correction as a new commit instead.
Hooks are safety guardrails, not friction — never bypass them; understand and fix what they flag, and treat --no-verify as needing fresh per-commit owner authorisation.
-n is the short alias of --no-verify (it bypasses pre-commit and commit-msg hooks); hooks are safety guardrails — never bypass them without fresh per-commit owner authorisation.
Commit signing is a safety guardrail, not friction — do not bypass it; resolve the signing setup rather than skipping the gate.
This silently discards uncommitted work — including parallel agents' edits — beyond fsck recovery; never use git to remove work, make forward-going filesystem changes instead.
This deletes untracked files irrecoverably, including peers' in-flight work; never use git to remove work, make forward-going filesystem changes instead.
This deletes untracked files irrecoverably, including peers' in-flight work; never use git to remove work, make forward-going filesystem changes instead.
This overwrites in-flight edits — including parallel agents' uncommitted work — beyond fsck recovery; never use git to remove work, make forward-going filesystem changes instead.
This overwrites in-flight edits — including parallel agents' uncommitted work — beyond fsck recovery; never use git to remove work, make forward-going filesystem changes instead.
This overwrites every in-flight edit in the tree — including parallel agents' uncommitted work — beyond fsck recovery; never use git to remove work, make forward-going filesystem changes instead.
Restore overwrites or unstages in-flight work — including parallel agents' uncommitted edits — beyond fsck recovery; never use git to remove work, make forward-going filesystem changes instead.
Restore overwrites or unstages in-flight work — including parallel agents' uncommitted edits — beyond fsck recovery; never use git to remove work, make forward-going filesystem changes instead.
Restore overwrites or unstages in-flight work — including parallel agents' uncommitted edits — beyond fsck recovery; never use git to remove work, make forward-going filesystem changes instead.
Never use git stash to park, hide, or discard work; keep in-progress work as live working-tree edits.
Never use git stash to park, hide, or discard work; keep in-progress work as live working-tree edits.
Stage by explicit pathspec so a commit never bundles parallel-thread or unrelated work; name the exact files.
Stage by explicit pathspec so a commit never bundles parallel-thread or unrelated work; name the exact files.
Stage by explicit pathspec so a commit never bundles parallel-thread or unrelated work; name the exact files.
Stage by explicit pathspec so a commit never bundles parallel-thread or unrelated work; git add -u stages every tracked modification and deletion in the tree — name the exact files.
Stage by explicit pathspec so a commit never bundles parallel-thread or unrelated work; git add --update stages every tracked modification and deletion in the tree — name the exact files.

The host is a shared substrate — unbounded load is a denial of service against the owner and every session. Provoke effects in-process (fake timers, injected delays); if load is truly needed it must be timeout-bounded, niced, announced, and reaped with a verified census.

preToolUse.description: Block shell commands that destroy history, bypass safety gates, overwrite live working-tree edits, discard stashed work, stage by wildcard, or saturate the shared host. Each entry carries a `concept` (the pattern family it is a fingerprint of), a `citation` (the doctrinal anchor), and a `reappraisal` (the positive direction the firing signals) so the deny message TEACHES: it names a concept to reappraise and steers the agent away from reaching for a sibling destructive command, rather than only refusing (PDR-044 §Innate immunity, as amended). The concepts: `history-destruction` (`git push --force` / `-f`, `git rebase -i`); `gate-bypass` (`git --no-verify`, `git --no-gpg-sign`); `worktree-destruction` (`git reset --hard`, `git clean -fd`, checkout/restore variants — these silently throw away in-flight edits, including peer-agent edits in parallel sessions, and `git fsck --lost-found` cannot recover them); `stash-discard` (`git stash drop` / `clear`); `wildcard-staging` (`git add -A` / `--all` / `.`, blocked under the explicit-pathspec discipline so a commit cannot accidentally bundle parallel-thread or unrelated work); `host-dos-unbounded-load` / `host-dos-forkbomb` (inline busy-loops, fork bombs, load tools — the host is a shared substrate and unbounded load is a denial of service against the owner and every session; these use `match: "substring"` because the shape hides inside one quoted token and a token-sequence trip cannot see into it; see `.agent/rules/no-unbounded-host-load.md`). We never use git to remove work; forward-going filesystem changes only. Entries may be bare strings (legacy) or objects; `concept` and `reappraisal` are optional at load time so a missing value never fails the guard closed (which would brick the worktree on a stale-dist/new-policy mismatch — a stale Bash guard blocks every command including the rebuild), and the `validate-policy-reappraisal` repo validator enforces their presence on object entries at commit-time. See `.agent/rules/never-use-git-to-remove-work.md`, `.agent/rules/no-verify-requires-fresh-authorisation.md`, `.agent/rules/stage-by-explicit-pathspec.md`, and `.agent/rules/no-unbounded-host-load.md`.

scoped_blocks.citation: PDR-044; principles.md §Core Philosophy: Engineering Excellence Over Speed
scoped_blocks.reappraisal: Re-assess whether the design is actually uniform — in which case state it positively — or whether you are about to encode expediency.

scoped_blocks.citation: Moving targets do not belong in permanent docs (PDR-079 portable surfaces only — ADRs are repo-bound and out of scope)
scoped_blocks.reappraisal: State the durable fact rather than this pointer; if a genuine historical reference is needed, mark it explicitly as one. The fix removes the dependence on a moving target.

scoped_blocks.citation: re-apply-first-question-at-elaboration-boundaries.md; principles.md §Core Philosophy: Engineering Excellence Over Speed
scoped_blocks.reappraisal: If you have analysed the situation to a verdict, present the verdict with its evidence; reserve owner questions for genuine permission gates and decisions only the owner can make.

scoped_blocks.citation: no-machine-local-paths.md; principles.md §No machine-local paths; org policy — no PII (a user-home absolute path leaks a username)
scoped_blocks.reappraisal: Use a repo-root-relative path for in-repo targets, or a platform-provided variable / tilde form (~/...) for per-user surfaces. A user-home or machine-temp absolute path resolves only on one machine and often leaks a username (PII). Portable system paths (/usr/bin, /opt/homebrew/bin) are fine; placeholder forms (/Users/<user>/) are fine.

preToolUseContent.description: Block agents from writing user-approved markers into file content (only the project owner can approve eslint-disable approvals with the JC: prefix). The path-scoped scoped_blocks are write-time fingerprints per PDR-044 §Innate immunity, grouped by `concept` so a citation and a positive `reappraisal` direction are authored once per concept rather than once per pattern; a firing teaches a concept to reappraise, not a word to rephrase. Four concept groups: (literal) `expediency-hedging` — language that means 'I know the rule applies, but this situation is special' rejected on doctrine surfaces (PDRs, plans, ADRs, governance), with doctrine-defining surfaces excluded so principles.md, distilled.md, PDR-043 and PDR-044 may catalogue the words they forbid; (regex) `sha-in-permanent-doc` — 7- to 40-character hex tokens with at least one a-f character on portable-permanent-doc paths (practice-core, rules, memory/active/patterns, principles.md, testing-strategy.md; ADRs are repo-bound and out of scope per PDR-079), with fenced code blocks, inline-code spans, and `(historical reference)` marker lines excluded so the discipline 'moving targets do not belong in permanent docs' (.agent/rules/no-moving-targets-in-permanent-docs.md) is enforced at write-time without tripping intentional code-block examples or explicit historical citations; (literal) `menu-framing` — owner-decision phrasings ('for owner verdict' / 'for owner decision' / 'for owner ratification') rejected on architectural surfaces ('for owner approval' was considered and rejected at design time — too high a false-positive rate in legitimate governance prose) so menu-shaped option-sets do not slip past the re-apply-first-question-at-elaboration-boundaries discipline; the rule file itself is excluded so it may catalogue the phrases it forbids; (regex) `machine-local-path` — user-home and machine-temp absolute paths (`/Users/<seg>`, `/home/<seg>`, `C:\Users\<seg>`, flattened `-Users-<seg>-` project ids, `/private/tmp`, `/var/folders`) blocked EVERYWHERE (`include_paths: [""]`; owner-directed invariant: the repo MUST NOT contain machine-local paths, a user-home path also leaks a username = PII), with the pattern-defining surfaces (no-machine-local-paths.md, important-state-not-in-temp-files.md, this policy file, the validator) excluded so they may name the patterns, and `archive/` directories excluded as frozen historical records (per the rule's Detection scope, which greps outside archive/); portable system paths and placeholder forms (`/Users/<user>/`) are exempt by construction (the regex requires a concrete segment); the same invariant is enforced over all tracked files at commit/CI by the `validate-no-machine-local-paths` repo-validator, which single-sources its pattern set from this block. `reappraisal` is optional at load time so a missing value never fails the guard closed; the `validate-policy-reappraisal` repo validator enforces its presence at commit-time. PDR-044 names false positives as a design property of the innate layer.

sessionStart.practice_box_check: Check .agent/practice-core/incoming/ for new Practice Core files.
sessionStart.description: Remind the agent to ground itself at session start.

preCommit.quality_gate_reminder: Run the required quality gates; `pnpm check` is the canonical aggregate gate.
preCommit.description: Remind the agent to run quality gates before committing.

claude_code.notes: Bash PreToolUse is natively activated for blocked-pattern enforcement. sessionStart and preCommit remain documented-only because grounding and quality-gate reminders already live in rules, skills, and onboarding flows.
